primary_sources.relevance: TUF requires clients to resist rollback, freeze, fast-forward, and mix-and-match attacks by retaining trusted metadata state and enforcing monotonic version relationships.
Certificate Transparency v2 formalizes append-only consistency as a distinct property from signature validity; this supports keeping a retained state anchor separate from packet authenticity.

finding: A valid signed witness-policy update is still replayable after restart unless the client retains a monotonic state anchor. Recovery must bind policy bytes, accepted sequence, predecessor state digest, and a caller-supplied minimum sequence or expected state digest.
design_implication: Persist the accepted policy sequence and state digest outside the fetched recovery packet. Reject equal, lower, skipped, predecessor-mismatched, tampered, or externally unanchored recovery states.

verification.method: dependency-free Node test run using the committed source content
covered_cases: sequence gap and predecessor mismatch rejected
stale and tampered recovery packets rejected

falsification_route: Demonstrate acceptance of a packet with sequence below the retained minimum, a broken predecessor digest, altered policy bytes, or a packet digest mismatch; any such acceptance falsifies the contract.
limits: The module does not provide durable storage or atomic filesystem writes.
Deleting or corrupting the external minimum-sequence or expected-digest anchor reopens rollback risk.
Digest continuity does not prove witness honesty, key custody, clock correctness, or policy truth.
No production witness network or recovery service was exercised.
